meeting_context: Regular meeting of the Mountain View Downtown Committee on October 4 focused on administrative actions, upcoming agenda planning, downtown development and hotel project status, Castro Street pedestrian mall progress, personal delivery device (PDD) pilot changes, committee vacancies/bylaws, community events, and maintenance/beautification priorities.

points.text: The committee approved the minutes for the August 9 and September 6 meetings following a motion by Maria Lange and a second by Jamil Shaikh; roll-call votes were recorded as ayes, and John confirmed the motion passed.
points.source: John Lang : All right, that passes.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Staff could not place the busking music item on this agenda and plans to bring that discussion to the November meeting. Staff also plans to return with a police update and the 2023 meeting calendar and dates in November.
points.source: John Lang : We weren't able...to get the busking music item on this particular agenda...work to get that conversation for the November meeting...We are looking to bring the Police Department to the November meeting...bring forward the 2023 calendar and meeting dates.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Staff will add downtown maintenance as agenda topic covering street cleaning and sidewalk maintenance issues.
points.source: John Lang : So I will list it as a maintenance of downtown going forward and I'll add that to the list of things.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: David Lin raised increased trash and litter on Castro Street tied to late-night activity and restaurant waste, requesting discussion of proposals to address it. Mike suggested a general downtown maintenance briefing; John agreed to add a maintenance topic and identify appropriate staff.
points.source: David Lin : ...trash kind of and litter on Castro... city staff is having trouble... either discussing a proposal to somehow address that... / Mike Kasperzak : ...opportunity for... a briefing on... downtown maintenance... / John Lang : ...I will list it as a maintenance of downtown... and I'll add that to the list.
points.speaker: David Lin

points.text: Maria Lange reported a Saturday night incident of public urination and conflicting, unlicensed street music, requesting police or community officer presence downtown and attention to unlicensed performers playing against each other.
points.source: Maria Lange : ...there was some pretty gross someone going to the bathroom in the street... wondering whether we could have some presence of police officer or community officer... conflicting music... unlicensed. So I'd like to see that addressed.
points.speaker: Maria Lange

points.text: Kira Pascoe said a consolidated list of holiday events is being prepared with the DBA. Halloween is planned for October 29. They are exploring a resident–business meet-up, an Elf on the Shelf activity, and another shopping activation. Initial details are expected by end of this week, with more next week.
points.source: Kira Pascoe : ...you will be getting a list of all the events... Halloween on the 29th of October... meeting local residents... as well as other holiday activities... Elf on a Shelf... first part by the end of this week and the second part by the end of next.
points.speaker: Kira Pascoe

points.text: Pamela requested updates on downtown ambassadors program and city-sponsored holiday events including tree lighting.
points.source: Pamela Baird : Can you also give us an update on the downtown ambassadors? Will that be brought back again during the holiday shopping season? And could you also give us an update on city sponsored events downtown
points.speaker: Pamela Baird

points.text: City Council approved 590 Castro Street (former Wells Fargo site south of City Hall) on August 30: a four-story, 106,000 square foot building with ground-floor retail and a public plaza.
points.source: Krisha Penollar : ...590 Castro Street... was approved at City Council on August 30th... a four story 106,000 square foot building with ground floor retail and a public Plaza.
points.speaker: Krisha Penollar

points.text: 954 Villa Street (former Tied House) obtained a provisional use permit for office. Temporary occupancy was issued, with minor fire issues being resolved; an office tenant is expected soon.
points.source: Krisha Penollar : ...954 Villa St.... provisional use permit to allow an office use and they did receive temporary occupancy... some minor fire issues... an office tenant will be taking over...
points.speaker: Krisha Penollar

points.text: 1110 Castro Street (former Zonz) submitted a plan and community permit and development review permit to upgrade the façades. A new restaurant, Vita, a Spanish tapas concept, will occupy the space.
points.source: Krisha Penollar : ...1110 Castro Street... we did receive a plan and community permit and development review permit to upgrade the facades and a new the restaurant called Vita... a Spanish tapas restaurant.
points.speaker: Krisha Penollar

points.text: Krisha Penollar delivered her final downtown update due to the role's rotation; Edgar will assume responsibilities as the new downtown planner for future committee updates.
points.source: John Lang : This will be Krisha's last one... Edgar will be taking over as the new downtown planner.
points.speaker: John Lang

topics.topic: Hope Street Lot hotel project status

points.text: The Hope Street Lot project exercised a DDA option to extend financing up to 40 months but has exhausted entitlement extensions; planning entitlements expire November 27. Without a building permit by then, the project must re-entitle. Staff expects more clarity next month; it does not appear on track.
points.source: John Lang : ...they have exercised an option... allows them to extend the financing... up to 40 months... planning entitlements... are going to expire on November 27th... If... no building permit... they have to re entitle... It... does not appear it's currently on track.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Pamela asked whether development streamlining (Matrix) was returning to Council on October 11. John stated that, to his knowledge, it was not scheduled for Council and staff are working internally, but staff will update the committee if that understanding is incorrect.
points.source: Pamela Baird : ...streamlining of projects... City Council October 11th. Will we be getting an update...? / John Lang : ...to my knowledge... it wasn't coming back on October 11th... internal work... We can update you if we're incorrect.
points.speaker: Pamela Baird

topics.topic: Castro Street pedestrian mall and lighting

points.text: On October 11, Council will open the public hearing on the Castro Street pedestrian mall, with ordinance adoption scheduled for October 25. The Business Improvement Assessment Area for next fiscal year will also be heard on October 11.
points.source: John Lang : ...on October 11th... the pedestrian mall will be going to Council for the first for the opening of the public hearing... heard on October 25th... the business improvement Assessment area is also going to City Council on the 11th.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Overhead cross-street lighting is largely complete, including the previously unfinished section on the 200 block. Due to tree canopy near midblock alleys, staff is exploring alternative cross-street lighting solutions. Staff are also working with businesses to remove tent remnants, broken planters, and dead plants.
points.source: John Lang : ...200 block of Castro Street... now completed... trees... don't allow for the existing lighting... looking for alternative ways to increase lighting... working with the businesses to get those items removed... declutter... getting rid of some of the broken planters and some of the dead plants.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Mike requested a Parks Division briefing on maintenance and trimming plans for the now-large downtown street trees and potential building constraints. John agreed to look into bringing this discussion back, noting larger limbs also create safety risks.
points.source: Mike Kasperzak : ...might be interesting to have somebody from the Parks Division come in and... maintenance and trimming of those trees... / John Lang : ...happy to look to bring that back... bigger trees means bigger limbs that can fall... I will note that and look into that.
points.speaker: Mike Kasperzak

points.text: The committee adopted the resolution authorizing continued virtual meetings under AB 361. John clarified the authorization must be renewed every 30 days, which is why it returns as a recurring item on the agenda.
points.source: Merry Yen : ...motion for the Downtown Committee to adopt the resolution... authorizing virtual meetings pursuant to 8361... / John Lang : ...It only lasts 30 days. So that's why it's a reoccurring item.
points.speaker: Merry Yen

points.text: Pamela supported remote format as suitable for committee's advisory role with less intensive discussion requirements.
points.source: Pamela Baird : I I personally am in favor of doing remote as opposed to previous commissions I've been on where there really is a lot of discussion and back and forth and meaty conversations. We tend to not do that because we're we don't.
points.speaker: Pamela Baird

points.text: Starship ended downtown operations over the summer. Other PDD providers have expressed interest in Mountain View but await finalized policy updates. Staff maintains a pilot framework to remain flexible as technology and operations evolve.
points.source: John Lang : ...we did have a provider operating in downtown... Starship... Over the summer, Starship... were no longer going to operate in downtown... other PDD operators... interested... they have not moved forward... because we have been discussing changes to the policy.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Proposed program changes include requiring a Mountain View presence beyond devices (e.g., office or maintenance facility) and enhanced device labeling with contact information for both the City and vendor.
points.source: John Lang : ...we want to make sure that there is a presence here in Mountain View... beyond just the device... include contact information for both the city and the vendor...
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Additional requirements would expand data and safety obligations: provide performance measures and regular reporting, document all public inquiries/conflicts, supply infrastructure condition feedback (e.g., sidewalk defects), and prohibit stopping in curb ramps or in front of pedestrian push buttons. Providers have been generally receptive.
points.source: John Lang : ...better performance measures... more regular reporting... document all public inquiries... providing some assessment on our infrastructure... augment the language to include not stopping in the curb ramp or in front of pedestrian push buttons... they are generally receptive to these changes.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: For remotely operated devices, the human handler requirement would be removed; it remains for fully autonomous units.
points.source: John Lang : ...remove the human handler... for those devices that are remotely operating... someone is actually physically controlling the device.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: To address arterial crossings, staff is considering predefined crossing routes to limit signal impacts and improve manageability. Staff is also exploring PDD-to-signal interaction options and noted occasional aerial delivery inquiries that have not advanced. Next, staff plans to return to BPAC and then Council for approval.
points.source: John Lang : ...identifying predefined routes... especially on large arterioles... considering dedicated paths... technology... where devices could trigger signals... we have... received a couple inquiries [about aerial]... go to BPAC... then... council for approval of changes.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Kira noted that delivery robots served as a draw during events and helped distinguish downtown. As the pedestrian mall evolves, such innovation can be a family-friendly attraction that supports brunch and lunch traffic and reinforces Mountain View's reputation as a hub of innovation.
points.source: Kira Pascoe : ...having the delivery droids... was actually kind of a tourist attraction... something that I know people always comment on... we want to be known more for brunch and for lunch... having something along those lines is worth thinking about having.
points.speaker: Kira Pascoe

points.text: Providers receptive to sharing general heat maps and incident data while protecting proprietary information.
points.source: John Lang : In most cases for the things we were asking data wise, it was not considered proprietary. We are not looking to get into their their their true IP.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: The Council Procedures Committee approved the Downtown Committee's bylaw changes to adjust Chamber/DBA representation; the next step is Council adoption. The related seat remains vacant until bylaws are finalized for transparency.
points.source: John Lang : ...the CPPC committee... did approve the recommendation... to augment the bylaws. So the next step is to take that now to council... that... seat will... remain empty until the bylaws are resolved.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Membership updates: Eric Cormier and Maria Lange will not continue next year. Marina Keith and David Lin intend to continue. Mike Kasperzak and Kira Pascoe intend to continue. Candidates have expressed interest for Maria's and Eric's seats. Jamil Shaikh continues as Neighborhood Association representative.
points.source: John Lang : ...Eric had... not be able to serve in the new year... Maria... will not be filling that seat... Mike and Kira... have expressed interest in serving again... there is a candidate that has expressed interest... potentially a candidate... for Eric's seat... Jamil fills the last seat.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Recruitment timeline: the Council Appointment Review Committee will interview candidates November 21, with Council appointments planned for December 6 or 13. John will email the dates and planning updates to the committee after the meeting.
points.source: John Lang : ...on November 21st will be doing the interview of candidates and then either December 6th or 13th is when council will do the appointing... I'll send... the dates noted in the presentation for everyone.
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Term length clarification: John initially stated two-year terms, but Merry Yen noted her experience reflects three-year staggered terms and that there is no limit on the number of terms members can serve.
points.source: Merry Yen : I think it's three... This is my third term in my 7th year... there's no limit to how many terms we can be new.
points.speaker: Merry Yen

points.text: Downtown trick-or-treating will return, including a bouncy house near Monte Carlo. A November 2 meet-and-greet with business owners is being organized (time TBD, breakfast or evening). Kira will share details this week and requested committee help promoting them in their communities.
points.source: Kira Pascoe : ...we are going to have trick or treating again... the bouncy House... by Monte Carlo... November 2nd, we're going to have a meet and greet with business owners... deciding... breakfast or an evening... will follow up... would also appreciate it if you could get the word out.
points.speaker: Kira Pascoe

points.text: The Mountain View Historical Association will conduct a Downtown Walking Tour on Sunday, October 23 at 2:00 PM. Registration is available on the association's website.
points.source: Pamela Baird : Downtown Walking Tour on Sunday, October 23rd at 2:00 PM... conducted by the Mountain View Historical Association... go to the Mountain View Historical Association website to register.
points.speaker: Pamela Baird

points.text: Only about 25% of downtown businesses currently exceed pre-pandemic sales, with roughly 75% still below. Staff is taking a flexible, soft approach to non-safety beautification issues, while expecting future pedestrian mall guidelines to increase uniformity.
points.source: John Lang : ...only 20, 5% of our businesses in downtown are actually doing better than they were pre pandemic. That means 75%... still way off sales... taking more of a softer approach... Under the pedestrian mall process... there'll be more guidelines...
points.speaker: John Lang

points.text: Merry Yen thanked Krisha Penollar for her work providing downtown development updates and planning support over multiple years. The meeting adjourned with a reminder for attendees to vote.
points.source: Merry Yen : ...Thank you Christian for all your work over the years. / Mike Kasperzak : ...We will be adjourned until next month... Don't forget to vote.
points.speaker: Merry Yen

decisions.text: Schedule the busking music discussion for the November Downtown Committee meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...I was not able to get the busking music item on this... I will keep that moving forward and hopefully work to get that conversation for the November meeting.

decisions.text: Bring a Police Department update to the committee's November meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...We are looking to bring the Police Department to the November meeting.

decisions.text: Present the 2023 Downtown Committee calendar and meeting dates at the November meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...looking to bring forward the 2023 calendar and meeting dates for this committee for 2023.

decisions.text: Add a downtown maintenance briefing to a future agenda and coordinate relevant staff to participate.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...I will list it as a maintenance of downtown going forward and I'll add that to the list of things.

decisions.text: Report back at the next committee meeting on the specific office tenant for 954 Villa Street.
decisions.source: Krisha Penollar : ...I don't have the specific office tenant handy, but I can have Edgar report back during next committee update.

decisions.text: Submit a mid-year budget request for Downtown Committee discretionary funds and plan to include a permanent allocation in the annual budget.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...drafting something for mid year... and then my idea is to roll that into a permanent thing for the annual.

decisions.text: Distribute a consolidated list of downtown holiday events (initial details by end of this week; further details by end of next week).
decisions.source: Kira Pascoe : ...you should be having that probably the first part by the end of this week and the second part by the end of next.

decisions.text: Explore bringing Parks Division staff to brief on downtown street tree maintenance and trimming at a future meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...happy to look to bring that back... I will note that and look into that.

decisions.text: Ask downtown businesses about impacts from Starship's departure and report findings to the committee.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...I'm happy to inquire... I think that's a great question, so I don't know the answer to it. I'm happy to inquire.

decisions.text: Take proposed PDD pilot revisions to BPAC and then to City Council for approval.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...take this to BPAC, assuming there's no other changes there. We'll go to council for approval of changes to the pilot program.

decisions.text: Email committee members the recruitment timeline dates and planning updates after the meeting.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...I'll send that as well as the dates noted in the presentation for everyone.

decisions.text: Contact the bicycle event organizer to check status and update the committee if there is news.
decisions.source: John Lang : I will reach out to him and... I'll put that out to the committee if there is any update to share.
